channel.title: Ninad Pathak
channel.description: Programmer-turned-writer helping DevTools and SaaS companies create technical content their engineering teams actually approve.

item.title: When to Hire a Technical Writer
item.description: Your engineering team cannot write everything. Know the signs that it is time to bring in specialized technical writing talent.

item.title: Building Content Hubs That Rank
item.description: Stop writing isolated blog posts. Build topical authority with interconnected content hubs that dominate search results.

item.title: Interviewing Engineers for Content
item.description: Extract knowledge from engineers effectively. Ask the right questions. Turn technical conversations into compelling content.

item.title: Repurposing One Technical Post Into Ten Assets
item.description: Maximize your content investment. Turn a single blog post into threads, videos, talks, documentation, and more without starting from scratch.

item.title: Writing Error Messages That Help Developers
item.description: Bad error messages waste engineering hours. Good error messages point directly to solutions. Learn to write the helpful kind.

item.title: The First 90 Days of a DevTools Content Strategy
item.description: Your first three months set the trajectory. Focus on quick wins, technical foundation, and building distribution before chasing perfection.

item.title: Creating Technical Diagrams That Do Not Confuse
item.description: Bad architecture diagrams waste time. Good diagrams explain systems instantly. Follow these principles for diagrams developers actually understand.

item.title: Product Led Content for DevTools
item.description: Stop writing generic thought leadership. Connect every piece of content to specific product capabilities your developers can try immediately.

item.title: Building a Developer Newsletter That Gets Opened
item.description: Developer inboxes are crowded. Here is how to build a newsletter developers actually read, from subject lines to content structure.

item.title: Measuring Content ROI for B2B SaaS
item.description: Stop tracking vanity metrics. Measure content performance by pipeline contribution, sales cycle impact, and customer acquisition cost.

item.title: The DevTools Content Audit Checklist
item.description: Audit your DevTools content strategy against 25 specific checkpoints. Find gaps. Fix priorities. Improve your editorial operation.

item.title: Writing Technical Content When You Are Not Technical
item.description: You do not need to be a senior engineer to write technical content. You need a process for learning fast and validating with experts.

item.title: The 30 Day Content Refresh Framework
item.description: Your old blog posts are assets. Refresh them systematically to regain lost traffic without writing from scratch.

item.title: Documentation Is Your Best Sales Page
item.description: Your API docs do more than explain functionality. They convert evaluators into users. Here is how to treat documentation as a growth channel.

item.title: Write Code Tutorials That Developers Actually Finish
item.description: Most code tutorials lose developers halfway through. Here is how to structure tutorials that keep readers engaged until the last line.
